description: Synthetic Swift eval corpus — exercises every Swift scoring path: overloads (SB-1 at the eval level), enclosing-type +10% signal, extension-target +15% signal, conformance edges, span-based identity, classifier initialism path. Mirrors fixtures/mixed-eval/'s shape. Acceptance per docs/swift-v1-handoff.md: ≥10/12 cases pass, MRR ≥ 0.85, negative case returns 0. Queries are designed to match literal substrings of code lines (rg invokes with -F).

cases.notes: Basic struct def lookup. Patient.swift must rank #1; PatientStore.swift is acceptable because it references Patient heavily.
PascalCase + initialism. Verifies the classifier's initialism regex (lib/classifier.js) accepts mixed AuthSession-style identifiers.

Multi-overload disambiguation by signature label. PatientStore.swift defines update(id:), update(patient:), and update(notes:for:); the literal `update(patient:` query must reach the patient overload line specifically.
Protocol def beats extensions on the same file. The `protocol View` line must rank above the `extension View` lines.
Protocol with associated types + async throws requirement. Exercises associatedtype path through the extractor.

The `+` token activates looksLikeExtensionQuery() (lib/scoring.js). View.swift contains a doc-comment anchor `/// SwiftUI-style View+Toolbar composition helpers.` immediately above the extension block. The +15% extension-target boost should fire on the extension def line within the same file.
Literal `extension` keyword path. Both View.swift's same-file extension and Toolbar.swift's separate-file extension must surface in top 5.

Multi-token query where one term (PatientStore) equals the enclosing type name. PatientStore.swift contains an in-class anchor comment `// numberOfPatients PatientStore — derived count`. The +10% SWIFT_ENCLOSING_TYPE_BOOST should fire on the anchor line because its `before` context exposes the `class PatientStore` declaration.
Init overload disambiguation via signature_hint. AuthSession defines init(user:) and init(token:refresh:). Query targets the second overload specifically.
Enum case with associated value. LogLevel.swift's `case error(String)` is a swift_declarations row with kind=case, parent_name=LogLevel.
Property declaration with attribute. The `@Published var patients: [Patient]` line is the canonical hit.
Garbage query across the Swift corpus. Must return zero results.

SB-1 invariant at the eval level. PatientStore.swift defines three update overloads — update(id:), update(patient:), update(notes:for:) — and retrieval must surface the file with multiple `func update` hits in close proximity. DB-level SB-1 verification (separate from this case): `sqlite3 fixtures/swift-eval/.planning/intel/graph.db "SELECT name, signature_hint, start_byte FROM swift_declarations WHERE name='update' AND parent_name='PatientStore' ORDER BY start_byte;"` must return exactly 3 rows with distinct signature_hint values (id:, patient:, notes:for:).
